titles.title: Macroscopic Schrödinger-cat states of nonequilibrium electrons induced by cat-state optical driving and projective measurements on the light field

raw_affiliations.value: Department of Physics, University of Tokyo, Hongo, Tokyo 113-0033, Japan

abstracts.value: We show that projective measurements on quantum light can induce macroscopic cat states in many-electron systems driven by such light. Here we investigate the quantum dynamics of <math><mi>N</mi></math> independent two-level electrons interacting with Schrödinger-cat or -kitten states of light. Without measurement, a macroscopic cat state of the electrons appears only in an ultrashort time window. In contrast, we demonstrate that photon-number parity or quadrature projective measurements can restore a macroscopic cat state in nonequilibrium electrons, even in the thermodynamic limit. These dynamics are captured by an external-field approximation, in which the electronic system evolves into a Rabi-oscillation cat state. Our results highlight the need for precise quantum measurement techniques for light to control macroscopic quantum states of matter driven by quantum light.

raw_refs.value: <ref id="c75"><label>[75]</label><mixed-citation publication-type="misc"><object-id>75</object-id>As an example of the backaction effect, Figs. <styled-content><xref ref-type="fig" rid="f4">4(c)</xref></styled-content> and <styled-content><xref ref-type="fig" rid="f4">4(f)</xref></styled-content> show that the QFI density gradually separates for the even- and odd-parity outcomes. A plausible interpretation is that a squeezed single-excitation Dicke-like state appears only for odd-parity outcomes, exhibiting greater quantumness than the unsqueezed one at <inline-formula><math><mrow><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mn>0</mn><mo>+</mo></msup></mrow></math></inline-formula>. Such spin squeezing is known to develop in coherent-field–driven Tavis–Cummings dynamics <styled-content><xref ref-type="bibr" rid="c115">[115]</xref></styled-content>.</mixed-citation></ref>

misc: As an example of the backaction effect, Figs. 4(c) and 4(f) show that the QFI density gradually separates for the even- and odd-parity outcomes. Such spin squeezing is known to develop in coherent-field–driven Tavis–Cummings dynamics [115]

raw_refs.value: <ref id="c78"><label>[78]</label><mixed-citation publication-type="misc"><object-id>78</object-id>For <inline-formula><math><mrow><mi>N</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></math></inline-formula> and an initial coherent state <inline-formula><math><mrow><mrow><mo>|</mo></mrow><msub><mi>α</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mrow><mo>〉</mo></mrow></mrow></math></inline-formula> (<inline-formula><math><mrow><msub><mi>α</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="double-struck">R</mi></mrow></math></inline-formula>), the next Born iteration (first backaction correction) yields the Heisenberg-picture field operator as <inline-formula><math><mrow><msub><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mi>BA</mi></mrow></msub><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>≈</mo><mrow><mo>[</mo><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mo>−</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>γ</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>/</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow><mspace width="0.16em"/><msup><mover accent="true"><mi>σ</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mi>y</mi></msup><mo>]</mo></mrow><mspace width="0.16em"/><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi>ω</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math></inline-formula> (keeping only the secular term linear in <inline-formula><math><mi>t</mi></math></inline-formula>). Equivalently, the Schrödinger-picture state is <inline-formula><math><mrow><mrow><mo>|</mo></mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">Ψ</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn><mi>BA</mi></mrow></msub><mrow><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>〉</mo><mo>≈</mo></mrow><msub><mover accent="true"><mi>U</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mi>XFA</mi></msub><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mspace width="0.16em"/><mover accent="true"><mi>D</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mrow><mo>(</mo><mo>−</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>γ</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>/</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow><msup><mover accent="true"><mi>σ</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mi>y</mi></msup><mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mspace width="0.16em"/><mo>|</mo><mo>↓</mo><mo>〉</mo><mo>|</mo></mrow><msub><mi>α</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mrow><mo>〉</mo></mrow></mrow></math></inline-formula>, with <inline-formula><math><mrow><msub><mover accent="true"><mi>U</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mi>XFA</mi></msub><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow></math></inline-formula> the XFA time-evolution operator and <inline-formula><math><mrow><mover accent="true"><mi>D</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>=</mo><mo form="prefix">exp</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><msup><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mo>†</mo></msup><mo>−</mo><mover><mi>α</mi><mo>¯</mo></mover><mover accent="true"><mi>a</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow></math></inline-formula> the displacement operator. The additional factor <inline-formula><math><mrow><mover accent="true"><mi>D</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mrow><mo>(</mo><mo>−</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>γ</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>/</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow><msup><mover accent="true"><mi>σ</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mi>y</mi></msup><mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow></math></inline-formula> produces a qubit-state–conditioned displacement of the light mode, implying light–matter entanglement even for a coherent-state drive and yielding a Gaussian collapse for the population dynamics, <inline-formula><math><mrow><mrow><mo>〈</mo><msup><mover accent="true"><mi>σ</mi><mo>̂</mo></mover><mi>z</mi></msup><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo>〉</mo></mrow><mo>=</mo><mo>−</mo><mo form="prefix">cos</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi><mi>eff</mi></msub><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mo form="prefix">exp</mo><mrow><mo>[</mo><mo>−</mo><msup><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>γ</mi><mi>ω</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>/</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>]</mo></mrow></mrow></math></inline-formula>, consistent with the <inline-formula><math><mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mo>|</mo></mrow><msub><mi>α</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mrow><mo>|</mo></mrow></mrow></math></inline-formula> expansion of the exact solution in Ref. <styled-content><xref ref-type="bibr" rid="c77">[77]</xref></styled-content>.</mixed-citation></ref>

misc: For N=1 and an initial coherent state |α0〉 (α0∈R), the next Born iteration (first backaction correction) yields the Heisenberg-picture field operator as âH,1BA(t)≈[â−i(γt/4)σ̂y]e−iωt (keeping only the secular term linear in t). Equivalently, the Schrödinger-picture state is |Ψ1BA(t)〉≈ÛXFA(t)D̂(−i(γt/4)σ̂y)|↓〉|α0〉, with ÛXFA(t) the XFA time-evolution operator and D̂(α)=exp(αâ†−α¯â) the displacement operator. The additional factor D̂(−i(γt/4)σ̂y) produces a qubit-state–conditioned displacement of the light mode, implying light–matter entanglement even for a coherent-state drive and yielding a Gaussian collapse for the population dynamics, 〈σ̂z(t)〉=−cos(Ωefft)exp[−(γωt)2/8], consistent with the 1/|α0| expansion of the exact solution in Ref. [77]
